entities.description: table is a specific logical type mentioned in the text that requires preservation of structural semantics
formula is a specific logical type mentioned in the text that requires preservation of structural semantics
v table is a distinct  typed entity representing the table itself created to preserve structural semantics
row is a component of table nodes that is explicitly extracted as a distinct entity
column is a component of table nodes that is explicitly extracted as a distinct entity
header refers to row and column headers in table nodes that are explicitly extracted as distinct entities
structural semantics refers to the meaning preserved for specific logical types in the described process
logical types are categories of entities  such as table and formula  that require specific handling
node refers to a specific point in the data structure where content is extracted
vertex refers to the primary node  v table  to which other entities are linked
containedin is the specific relationship type used to link row and column headers to the table entity

relations.description: v table is the distinct entity created to represent the table logical type
row headers are linked to v table via a  containedin  relationship
column headers are linked to v table via a  containedin  relationship
row and column headers are explicitly extracted and linked to v table
structural semantics are preserved specifically for logical types like table and formula
v table is created as a distinct entity from the content of a specific node
row headers are a specific type of header extracted from table nodes
column headers are a specific type of header extracted from table nodes
row and column headers are both explicitly extracted components of table nodes
